name: Dan Greaney
headline: Filed FEC Form 2 — declared 2028 campaign
why: Only formally declared major-party filer so far; low-profile.

name: Gregory "Greg" Bovino
headline: "All options" exploratory posture for 2028
why: Early exploratory lane on the MAGA-enforcement flank.

name: Jo Jorgensen
headline: Formed exploratory committee for 2028 LP nomination
why: Most concrete step on the Libertarian side.

name: Gavin Newsom
headline: "Lying if I said I wasn't seriously thinking about it"
why: Catapulted to front-runner in Emerson national polling.
quote: I'd be lying if I said I wasn't seriously thinking about it.

name: Kamala Harris
headline: Has not decided; "may or may not" run again
why: Retains 2024 donor & staff network; leads some rematch polls.
quote: I have not made a decision… I may or may not.

name: Cory Booker
headline: "Of course I'm thinking about it" — not ruled out
why: Most explicit yes among Senate Democrats; record-floor-speech visibility.
quote: Of course I'm thinking about it.

name: Andy Beshear
headline: Open "under the right circumstances"; decision after DGA term
why: Red-state Dem appeal; timeline tied to Governors Assoc. chairmanship.
quote: I'd consider it under the right circumstances.

name: Mark Kelly
headline: Reported to be "seriously considering" a 2028 run
why: Swing-state, astronaut/veteran profile draws operative interest.

name: Ro Khanna
headline: "We'll look at that after the midterms"
why: First clear public timeline from the progressive-pragmatist lane.
quote: We'll look at that after the midterms.

name: Ruben Gallego
headline: Shifted from "no" to "we have to look at it"
why: Notable upgrade — went from denial to open in weeks.
quote: We have to look at it.

name: J.B. Pritzker
headline: "Can't rule anything out" for 2028
why: Self-funding capacity removes the money constraint entirely.

name: Rahm Emanuel
headline: Publicly positioning / testing a centrist lane
why: Aggressive media tour to project vitality and viability.

name: Josh Green
headline: Among Dems considering; "open if needed"
why: Outside-lane physician-governor; low national profile.

name: Mitch Landrieu
headline: Quietly exploring / soft-launching a possible bid
why: Behavioral signals (travel, donor outreach) ahead of any quote.

name: Elissa Slotkin
headline: Not set on running, but "keeping her name in the mix"
why: Most recent signal on the board; swing-state freshman senator.

name: Ted Cruz
headline: Weighing another run — setting up a clash with Vance
why: 2016 infrastructure & donor base; explicit intra-MAGA lane.

name: Ron DeSantis
headline: Didn't rule it out: "We'll see… you never know"
why: 2024 national org still partly intact; term-limited in 2026.

name: Rand Paul
headline: "Thinking about it" — puts odds around 50-50
why: Libertarian-Republican lane; rare explicit probability.

name: Tulsi Gabbard
headline: Would "never rule out" an opportunity to serve
why: Admin platform raises visibility; non-committal framing.
quote: I'd never rule out an opportunity to serve.

name: Don Bacon
headline: Won't rule out governor — or president — in 2028
why: Centrist Republican lane; also weighing NE governor.

name: Don Lemon
headline: Says he is "seriously considering" a run
why: No party structure or office base — celebrity lane.

name: Pete Buttigieg
headline: Hinted at 2028 but stays noncommittal
why: Top-3 in Dem polls on name ID; no explicit "considering" yet.

name: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez
headline: Hasn't ruled out higher office; frames ambition as policy
why: Massive small-dollar base; leads some progressive polls.

name: Josh Shapiro
headline: Cautious, but wants to be "part of the national debate"
why: Marquee swing-state governor; deliberately low-key.

name: Phil Murphy
headline: "Nothing off the table," while downplaying the odds
why: Term-ended governor; low probability self-assessment.

name: Andrew Yang
headline: Reported mulling 2028; says odds of running are high
why: Third-party/independent lane outside the two primaries.

name: Afroman (Joseph Foreman)
headline: Hinted at a 2028 Libertarian run
why: Celebrity LP lane; no committee yet.

name: J.D. Vance
headline: Downplays it — "I'm a VP and I like my job"
why: Leads GOP primary polls despite no "considering" language; Trump floats a Vance-Rubio ticket.
quote: I'm not a potential future candidate. I'm a Vice President.

name: Gretchen Whitmer
headline: Ruled it out, then walked it back ("never say never")
why: Contradictory signals — flagged for source conflict.

name: Steve Bannon
headline: Refuses to rule out a run; posture muddy
why: Tied up with Trump-third-term advocacy; no structure.

name: Michael Flynn
headline: Conditional: would run only if Mike Pence runs
why: Pure contingency — not an independent signal.

name: Tucker Carlson
headline: Mused about 2028, didn't fully rule out — no structure
why: Speculative; included for completeness, low signal value.

name: Hunter Biden
headline: Flirts publicly: "Let's take another crack with a Biden"
why: Joke/troll posting, incl. VP talk with Newsom — not a real candidacy.
quote: Let's take another crack with a Biden.

name: Donald Trump
headline: Barred by 22nd Amendment; teases third-term scenarios
why: Legally ineligible; tracked only because of the chatter he generates.

name: Marco Rubio
headline: Not planning a run; signals support for Vance
why: Trump floats him as Vance's running mate, not a rival.

name: Wes Moore
headline: Explicitly says he is not running in 2028
why: Clear denial; often cited as a future-cycle prospect instead.

name: Gina Raimondo
headline: Said yes, then later reporting: no longer considering
why: Cooled off after initial openness.

name: Tim Walz
headline: Earlier "may run," later "not thinking about 2028"
why: 2024 VP nominee who walked back earlier interest.

name: Marjorie Taylor Greene
headline: TIME reported private interest; she publicly denied it
why: Source conflict resolved toward denial.

name: Stephen A. Smith
headline: Entertained a run, later said no plans
why: Backed away from earlier flirtation.
